121458.explanation: You choose leaders based on quality and suitability. Equal opportunities are important; fixed quotas are not.

121459.explanation: Residents must be well involved, but a referendum is not always the best tool. Sometimes consultation or participation works better.

121462.explanation: Entrepreneurial freedom is important, but not automatic. It must fit the area and comply with rules.

121464.explanation: Use may continue, but with attention to health and air quality. Clear rules are needed.

121465.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality and costs a lot of money. The nuisance and costs do not always justify the effect.

121467.explanation: Accessibility is important, but assistance must be targeted. Entrepreneurs remain primarily responsible themselves.

121468.explanation: Small-scale construction can help with housing shortages and family succession, as long as it fits the village and the rules.

121471.explanation: Culture is valuable, but this is not a core municipal task. Schools and parents make these choices.

121478.explanation: This causes extra pressure on facilities and enforcement. Holiday homes are not meant for this.

121479.explanation: Building on the outskirts may be necessary to address housing shortages, provided the village character is preserved.

121480.explanation: Those who can participate, should participate. A reciprocal obligation helps with structure, self-esteem, and contact.

121481.explanation: No automatic waiver. Assistance must be targeted, but everyone contributes according to their ability.

121482.explanation: Assistance is intended for those who really need it. An increase may lead to higher costs and less customization.

121485.explanation: No standard 30 km/h speed limit. The speed must fit the road and its design.

121486.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but not unlimited. Care must remain accessible, efficient, and affordable.

121489.explanation: Abolishing it is appealing, but costs money. First, it must be clear if this is financially feasible.

121490.explanation: An increase affects tourism and entrepreneurs. We must be cautious with rising charges.

121493.explanation: In case of scarcity, priority must go to our own residents and people who work here.

121495.explanation: Solar panels should mainly be on roofs. Agricultural land is scarce and valuable.

121496.explanation: Swimming skills are important, but responsibility primarily lies with parents.

121499.explanation: High-rise flats do not fit the village character of Oostzaan. We build in harmony with the surroundings.

121501.explanation: Parking pressure is high. People must be able to park their car close to home.

121504.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality and provides little added value.

121506.explanation: Twiske Poort offers opportunities for sports and youth, provided nature and the village are protected.

121507.explanation: Events are possible within clear agreements about noise and nature protection.

121458.explanation: A council with as many men as women brings together different experiences and perspectives. This leads to better decisions and a government in which more people can identify.

121459.explanation: GroenLinks values participation highly and initiated the establishment of a Citizens' Advisory Council (BAR). Citizen input can take many forms, and since Oostzaan has no referendum regulation, we do not consider a referendum the best tool.

121462.explanation: Extra activities do not always fit with nature, special provincial landscapes, and the living environment. Tailored solutions are needed: space for farmers where possible, but only if done carefully and without burdening the surroundings.

121464.explanation: Wood burning produces a lot of fine dust and has serious negative health effects. People with lung diseases such as asthma or COPD, the elderly, and children suffer extra from this.

121465.explanation: The municipality can facilitate a fireworks show, for example by COE, but does not have to organize it itself.

121467.explanation: Accessibility is essential for an inclusive society. However, the municipality's financial space is very limited, so support must be targeted and fair.

121468.explanation: There is limited space in Oostzaan due to the Special Provincial Landscape and Natura2000. Therefore, GroenLinks is cautious about new construction but supports living on a parent's or mother's property: small-scale, integrated with the landscape, and without harming nature and openness.

121471.explanation: We consider music education very important, but municipal finances must allow for it.

121478.explanation: This is actually not applicable to Oostzaan.

121479.explanation: We must first build within the village boundaries and handle green space and landscape carefully.

121480.explanation: Mandatory reciprocal obligations put people under pressure, while not everyone is able to do this due to health or personal circumstances. Trust, customization, and good guidance work better.

121481.explanation: Waiving fees gives residents with low incomes relief. It prevents people from getting into financial trouble and contributes to equal opportunities and security of existence in the municipality.

121482.explanation: Inflation and energy poverty are still a problem, also in Oostzaan.

121485.explanation: 30 km/h zones help make streets safer and more pleasant. Even if not everyone drives exactly 30, people generally slow down. This creates more calm, fewer serious accidents, and more space for pedestrians, cyclists, and a pleasant neighborhood.

121486.explanation: When people feel understood and respected in their faith, culture, or beliefs, care works better. Freedom of choice increases trust, equality, and accessibility for everyone.

121489.explanation: The revenues go into the general funds, enforcement is relatively expensive and the income is limited. Abolishing it is fairer and simpler for both residents and the municipality.

121490.explanation: A higher tourist tax ensures that visitors fairly contribute to maintenance and cleaning. This allows the municipality to invest in livability and nature, while residents benefit from it.

121493.explanation: This measure helps people with a residence permit get a safe start quickly. Permanent housing accelerates integration, access to work, and education.

121495.explanation: Where this is possible and desirable. This must not come at the expense of nature or valuable landscapes.

121496.explanation: Swimming skills are incredibly important in this water-rich environment.

121499.explanation: Housing construction must fit within the surroundings. This usually means: two stories and a pitched roof.

121501.explanation: More parking spaces often come at the expense of greenery and play areas. By choosing fewer cars and more space for walking, cycling, and socializing, neighborhoods become healthier, safer, and more pleasant for everyone.

121504.explanation: By working together with Ducktopia, we invest in a local, animal-friendly solution. A local initiative that knows the animals and environment ensures careful care and a pleasant and clean living environment for both people and animals.

121506.explanation: GroenLinks is not necessarily against developing Twiske Poort, but it should be nature-inclusive, small-scale, and fit with Het Twiske. The current plans are not. Oostzaan should also be able to decide independently about developments on our territory, not only the recreation board.

121507.explanation: Large festivals should not be held in a Natura 2000 area, especially not during the breeding season.

121458.explanation: 'Striving for' an equal distribution is more realistic than 'must'. Parties forming a coalition from March onwards have it in their own hands.

121459.explanation: The CDA wants to establish an official Village Council in the short term. A representative group of Oostzaan residents who can express their opinion, solicited or unsolicited, to the municipal council. Faster, clearer, more nuanced, and cheaper than having to organize a referendum each time.

121462.explanation: Yes, if this helps to continue farming actively, but in terms of the shop only with farm-related products, preferably organic. Regarding a campsite, only if it is small-scale, natural, and without nuisance to the neighborhood.

121464.explanation: Yes, with information about responsible use, including using clean and dry wood to limit smoke formation. Also, consideration can be given to introducing or advising a burn ban within the built-up area at certain times.

121465.explanation: Yes, combined with a New Year's Eve party where Oostzaan residents can meet each other.

121467.explanation: Yes, for municipal buildings and school buildings; for buildings of private enterprises, that responsibility lies with the entrepreneur.

121468.explanation: Building in the second line from the built-up area or on former industrial sites can be attractive to help solve the housing shortage. Building on open land yourself leads to clutter and damage to peat meadow and nature areas. Also, the infrastructure there is not designed for that.

121471.explanation: Music education is enjoyable for many children, just like sports. Subsidies for every child are not necessary.

121478.explanation: Only as long as there is a housing shortage and under strict conditions and supervision, such as only for part of a park to keep space for recreation. The scheme should therefore be temporary.

121479.explanation: Certainly now, with housing scarcity, helping in small ways, e.g. 1. new construction on former industrial sites in Oostzaan, 2. living in the second line of a ribbon development, can be additions that bind more people to Oostzaan and help local facilities to survive.

121480.explanation: A social assistance benefit acts as a social safety net for people who need it. That's good. If a recipient, in return, commits to socially meaningful work, another resident can be helped as well. That is community living.

121481.explanation: As long as the tax system, both nationally and locally, still works with exemptions and/or allowances, it shows solidarity to financially support people with a low income. An important boundary in this support remains that work must pay off.

121482.explanation: Tailored solutions are a good answer to severe cases. Increasing the income threshold broadly sounds noble but is not always necessary and, given the municipality's tight finances, quickly unaffordable.

121485.explanation: Within the built-up area there is usually a mix of pedestrians, cyclists, and cars. There, 30 km/h contributes to safety. Outside the built-up area, especially with separate bike paths, it is different.

121486.explanation: Care and assistance are often urgently needed; then 'always being able to choose' can become life-threatening. Care and assistance that are reimbursed are in principle of a general nature.

121489.explanation: The (administrative) costs to levy the dog tax are essentially lost tax money. The current net revenue can be collected more effectively via property tax.

121490.explanation: The tourist tax in Oostzaan is well above the North Holland average. A measure that yields relatively little and possibly raises the threshold for filing a declaration.

121493.explanation: Every municipality has a task to accommodate status holders, but in times of housing shortage, like now, the municipality can, out of solidarity with other housing seekers, also come up with temporary solutions for housing status holders. Think of the accommodation for Ukrainians.

121495.explanation: First use other options, such as panels on roofs of municipal buildings and business premises, or insulation of buildings, then consider this.

121496.explanation: In water-rich Oostzaan, learning to swim at a young age is vital. Group swimming lessons are also good for bonding among children at school and for more physical activity for young children.

121499.explanation: Because the possibilities to build homes in Oostzaan are extremely limited, the CDA is in favor of medium-rise buildings, as the zoning plan sometimes already allows (up to 5 floors). The result is more homes for Oostzaan residents in the village, also good for shops and facilities.

121501.explanation: Whether temporary or not, to provide relief in places where there is parking pressure, for example near new construction or because children (with cars) have to live at home longer due to lack of housing. Preference is to use water-permeable tiles.

121504.explanation: The muscovy ducks are a well-known problem in large numbers in water-rich Oostzaan; moreover, they pollute public spaces. Capturing them is costly and akin to bailing out a leaking boat.

121506.explanation: The goal of Twiske Poort is to give the Twiske more recreational appeal. The Amsterdam region wants this, but what is the balance between burdens and benefits for Oostzaan when there is extra traffic, increased noise, and crowding for much of the year?

121507.explanation: There is no need to organize 'large' festivals in the Twiske. The Amsterdam region wants this, but what is the balance between burdens and benefits for the nature area and Oostzaan? The CDA wants first a thorough evaluation from Oostzaan's perspective in the interest of the residents.

121458.explanation: Everyone is equal at the PvdA.

121459.explanation: We at the PvdA find the opinion of all Oostzaan residents very important. They should be part of very important issues to be able to participate in decision-making.

121465.explanation: This would be a very good alternative to address the disturbances during New Year's Eve.

121467.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate everywhere, so here too we support that people with disabilities should be able to access all places, and if as a municipality we can help entrepreneurs with that, we fully support it.

121471.explanation: Music lessons at school are very important for every child.

121481.explanation: We as the PvdA want people with a low income to be able to live a normal life. We can help them by having the municipality waive certain taxes.

121496.explanation: We as the PvdA find it very important that children learn to swim at a young age.

121499.explanation: We as the PvdA want to build more in Oostzaan.

121504.explanation: We support this initiative. If this can limit the population.

121507.explanation: As long as they meet all the standards set, we as the PvdA do not mind if one festival remains in the Twiske.

121458.explanation: Aldermen come from multiple parties. In addition, administrative experience and knowledge of Oostzaan and the Zaanstreek-Waterland region are important.

121459.explanation: D66 supports democratic renewal as a supplement to representative democracy. Referenda and citizen advisory councils are a good addition.

121462.explanation: Always is too definite. Shops are really something different than campsites. For campsites, issues like nature, traffic, nitrogen emissions, and neighbors also play a role.

121464.explanation: With the advice to follow the recommendations of the “Stookwijzer” (Heating Guide).

121465.explanation: Contributes to solidarity and community spirit.

121468.explanation: Building on “parents’ land” is a reasonable alternative. Building on agricultural land according to the spatial vision.

121471.explanation: Cultural education in a broad sense enhances children's development.

121479.explanation: Use all possible housing construction locations.

121480.explanation: For those who are capable, with guidance as a stepping stone towards work and education.

121481.explanation: Waiving debts prevents indebtedness and stress.

121482.explanation: An income limit of 120% of the minimum ensures that working poor and groups just above the minimum do not fall through the cracks. Minimizing the poverty trap as much as possible.

121485.explanation: 30 km/h is good for traffic safety and results in significantly fewer injuries.

121486.explanation: Always is too definite. This can conflict with the availability of care.

121489.explanation: Dog tax is a general levy without a direct service in return.

121490.explanation: Higher tourist tax can harm the local economy.

121493.explanation: Humane reception and rapid integration are necessary, but on the housing market a fair distribution system with other groups of housing seekers is needed.

121495.explanation: Depends on the local situation. Preference goes to rooftops and facades.

121496.explanation: In the interest of equal opportunities and safety.

121499.explanation: Maintain the current building heights of 3 floors with a roof.

121501.explanation: Current number of parking spaces in neighborhoods is sufficient. When expanding the number of homes in a neighborhood, also provide parking spaces.

121504.explanation: With proper management and prevention, a reception location in the municipality of Oostzaan is not necessary.

121506.explanation: Previously on the same location was De Blauwe Poort.

statements.title: The municipality should continue to allow large festivals in the recreation and nature area Het Twiske.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
Residents of Oostzaan should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Oostzaan moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should fund a new shelter for muscovy ducks.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
In Oostzaan the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Oostzaan moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: High-rise residential towers may be built in Oostzaan.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Oostzaan must block the construction of Twiske Poort.
